title: SIA-W+H lever attribution — when harness vs weight update

rung2_hard.verdict: base BELOW floor, invalid_json 0 -> real headroom not artifact

ladder_run_nebius.serving_solved: Path-2 GPU VM. Nebius 1xH200 (195.242.31.45) vLLM 0.22.1 serving openai/gpt-oss-120b MXFP4 + attention-only lever_lora. Provision->serve->eval proven end-to-end.
ladder_run_nebius.rung2_base_vs_lora_3step: NEGATIVE/undertrained: base_acc 0.333 vs lora_acc 0.292 (delta -0.042), regret 0.124->0.094, invalid_json 0, fixed 0 regressed 1. 3-step LoRA (3ep,bs8,lr1e-5,packing) installs no attribution skill. NOT a win; regret-only drop insufficient.
ladder_run_nebius.rung2_retrain_20ep: DEGENERATE majority-collapse. ftjob-044cd47fb30140c8bba750371262ff7c (20ep lr1e-4): LoRA predicts H for ALL 24 eval episodes. acc 0.458 = gold-H-rate 11/24 (majority baseline BY DEFINITION), regret WORSE 0.161->0.202. NOT attribution. 3-step(lr1e-5)=undertrained 0.29, 20ep(lr1e-4)=collapse 0.458; pure SFT on 48 ex has no stable middle. base 0.333 at least varies actions.
ladder_run_nebius.vm_state: KEPT WARM per user 2026-06-06. computeinstance-e00z3d8h6txgkwy3f6 @195.242.31.45, ~$4.63/hr idle, vLLM serving base+lever_lora(20ep). Delete: nebius compute instance delete --id computeinstance-e00z3d8h6txgkwy3f6
ladder_run_nebius.rung2_balanced_v2_REAL_WIN: ftjob-86ba821743d64fdeac8da782e238709e (balanced 189ex 63/63/63, lr3e-5, valid_loss 0.046). acc 0.542 > base 0.333 AND > best-constant 0.458; regret CRUSHED 0.161->0.043; action_dist H_THEN_W10/H4/W10 NON-collapsed (~gold). collapse_report.py verdict REAL_WIN (all 4 checks). First genuine LoRA attribution win on HARD. Below ceiling 0.81 (room remains). Plot results/rung2_comparison.png. Eval UNCHANGED (hard_cache seed2), no leakage (validate_dataset.py OK). Collapse was DATA artifact (tiny+H-imbalanced) not model limit.
ladder_run_nebius.anti_collapse_gate: gpt_oss/eval/collapse_report.py: constant-policy baselines (always H/W/H_THEN_W) + collapse flag (>80% one action) + REAL_WIN only if beats base AND all constants AND regret not worse AND not collapsed. Caught the 20ep false-win.
ladder_run_nebius.iteration_speedups: rollout_parallel.py (24ep ~3s vs ~4min), vLLM hot-swap (VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=1 + POST /v1/load_lora_adapter, instant no-restart), train_lora_vm.py local QLoRA on MXFP4 base (deps kernels==0.12.0; blocked on transformers5.10.2 kernel-hub glitch, try DISABLE_KERNEL_MAPPING=1; TF is fallback). Caveat: never concurrent scp same path (truncates).
ladder_run_nebius.blocker_resolved: was: TF custom-weights BETA. Solved via self-hosted vLLM on Nebius compute VM.
ladder_run_nebius.auto_eval_ready: scripts/driver_lora_eval.sh <job_json> <cache> <tag> once a served LoRA model id exists
ladder_run_nebius.rung3_4_status: demos only (trimul_harness.py / codeopt_lever_demo.py) — need hard_task-style cache builders before they become LoRA rungs

pivot.title: SIA-Lever-120B: Learning When to Update Harness vs Weights
pivot.status: scaffold complete + CPU-validated; blocked on GPU/endpoint for gpt-oss-120b base eval + LoRA.

rigor_pass_result: PASS (paper-level). Phase 1: 15 seeds + Welch t (shortcut_sens d=16.3 p=1.6e-16; composition d=2.58; identity d=2.17; inverse d=1.32), gate 15/15. Metric suite upgraded: equivariance proxy -> group-axiom identity/inverse errors. Phase 3: 10 seeds/30 ep, selector regret 0.014 (acc 0.97), lowest at EVERY W_COST incl 0.00 (sweep), Wilcoxon p=0.013; measurement separated from W_COST scoring. Leak sweep: shortcut-cheat adversarial (only alpha=1.0); H->W group-repair holds all alpha. Wording fixed (preserve/repair). Provenance + writeup docs added.
phase_0_result: PASS — prediction-only learner exploits shortcut (clean_mse~0, neg_control low, composition_err~5.6). Shortcut win real.
phase_1_result: PASS 5/5 seeds — W-only keeps shortcut_win; H->W -> clean_win (neg_control 0.23->1.06, composition 5.6->0.002, shortcut_sens 1.07->0.002). Table+plot in results/.
phase_2_result: PASS — subagent authored structural verifier from weak harness + trace. cheater flagged 5/5, honest 0/5. Diff: figures/harness_update.diff.
phase_3_result: PASS — 3 real failure modes (shortcut_leak->H_THEN_W, model_prior_gap->W, bad_verifier->H). Oracle-sandwich selector regret 0.046 vs alternating 0.116, H_only 0.235, W_only 0.438 (3 seeds, real reruns, no transition table). Table+plot in results/.
phase_4_blocker: Gemma LoRA weight-update needs GPU (hackathon). Local Gemma LLM-selector needs ollama model pull — paused; user rejected pull. Rule selector already proves the claim. AWAITING: GPU access or user go-ahead to pull a local model.
publication_goal: Hackathon demo (one-day). Research-track angle: lever attribution in self-improving agents.
core_claim: Weight-only updates can reinforce shortcut-cheating; harness-first-then-weight (H->W) fixes it. Proven once, measured, on a 2D rotation + shortcut-channel toy.
anti_goals: No Gemma LoRA as load-bearing dependency.
No 200-episode benchmark before the single real loop is measured.

phases.PHASE_0: Toy + measurement. Real shortcut-win confirmed (low clean_mse + low neg_control_mse).
phases.PHASE_1: Four-stage episode: prediction-only -> W-only -> H update -> H->W. Measured deltas. THE WIN.
phases.PHASE_2: Agentic H: Claude Code patches verifier from failed trace (real diff).
phases.PHASE_3: Lever selector over 2-3 REAL failure modes, regret by real reruns.

exit_gates.PHASE_0: prediction-only model: low clean_mse AND low neg_control_mse (shortcut win is real).
exit_gates.PHASE_1: stage-4 neg_control_mse > stage-2 neg_control_mse, real reruns. If only this lands, project is a win.
exit_gates.PHASE_2: visible agent-produced verifier diff + detection works on patched harness.
exit_gates.PHASE_3: rule-selector beats H-only and W-only on >=3 real episodes, measured regret.

next_action.reason: Phases 0-3 done + measured. Phase 4 blocked on GPU (Gemma LoRA) / user decision (local Gemma pull). Core claim fully proven without it.
next_action.hint: Demo path: python experiments/run_seeds.py (Phase 0/1 win), python harness/verifier.py (Phase 2 agentic-H), python experiments/phase3.py (Phase 3 selector). All CPU, ~2-3 min total.

needs_user_input_phase4.prompt: Phase 4 (Gemma) needs either GPU for LoRA weight-update, or approval to pull a local ollama Gemma for an LLM-selector garnish. Core result complete without it — proceed with Phase 4, or stop here?

execution: local CPU only. Tiny MLP, seconds per train. No SLURM, no GPU, no cluster.

needs_user_input.prompt: Path-2 GPU-VM serving is turnkey except interactive Nebius CLI auth (2FA). Run: ! ~/.nebius/bin/nebius profile create  then set HF_TOKEN and call scripts/nebius_serve_lora.sh. ~$30-40/hr for 8xH200 — delete VM when done. vLLM-LoRA-on-MoE is the main risk (fallback: merge adapter).

active_runs.fix_note: 19688952 FAILED: vLLM 0.22.1 pulled torch 2.11.0+cu130 (needs driver>=13) but seas_gpu H200 driver=12.9. Repaired venv with torch 2.11.0+cu129 (with deps); sbatch patched to force cu129. Resubmitted as 19693375.
active_runs.description: Harvard-cluster parallel path to Nebius serving blocker: vLLM-serve gpt-oss-120b (native MXFP4, 63GB, 1x H200) + hard-rung LoRA on localhost, run base-vs-LoRA selector eval on hard_cache. First-to-serve wins vs Nebius Path-2.
active_runs.gate: rung2 base 0.33 -> LoRA beats floor 0.46 toward ceiling 0.81 = real attribution result
active_runs.risk: vLLM gpt-oss MoE-LoRA serving (needs vllm>=0.15); ungated HF pull of openai/gpt-oss-120b (fallback: HF_TOKEN)

completed_runs.result: 5/5 gate pass. W-only keeps shortcut_win; H->W -> clean_win.
selector regret 0.046 vs alternating 0.116, H_only 0.235, W_only 0.438. Lever acc 0.89.
completed_runs.config: 3 seeds, 800 steps/train, W_COST=0.05 (superseded by rigor pass)

completed_runs.result: gate 15/15. Welch t W-only vs H->W: shortcut_sens p=1.6e-16 d=16.3; composition p=5.7e-6 d=2.58; identity d=2.17; inverse d=1.32.
selector regret 0.014 acc 0.97 (29/30); lowest at every W_COST incl 0.00 (0.019); Wilcoxon vs alternating p=0.013.
completed_runs.config: 10 seeds, 800 steps, W_COST sweep {0,0.01,0.03,0.05,0.10}, measurement separated from scoring

completed_runs.result: shortcut-cheat only at alpha=1.0 (adversarial); H->W group-repair (composition ~0.002) holds at every alpha.

events.detail: Project created via /make-project. Phenomenon-first scope locked after critique of LeverBench-first plan (circular oracle, synthetic traces).
Toy built + strengthened (full-vector shortcut leak). 4-stage episode, 5 seeds: gate 5/5. Phenomenon measured. Table+plot saved.
Phase 2: subagent authored structural verifier (figures/harness_update.diff). Phase 3: oracle-sandwich selector regret 0.046 beats fixed policies on 3 real failure modes. Phase 4 blocked on GPU/user.

Paper-level rigor: group-axiom metrics, 15-seed Phase 1 + Welch t, 10-seed Phase 3 + W_COST sweep (selector wins at cost 0.00 too) + Wilcoxon, leak-strength sweep (honest two-finding), provenance + writeup. Wording corrected to preserve/repair.

Full adversarial audit. CPU core REPRODUCES exactly (Phase 0/1/2/3 match; Phase-2 diff byte-reproducible). No private-data leakage; train/eval split disjoint by seed (0-6 vs 7-9 = published private eval). Bugs fixed: evaluator crash on malformed JSON -> fail-soft; silent duplicate dedupe -> first+flag; provenance missing harness/evaluator/adapter hashes -> added; check_env HF_TOKEN display bug; rollout_adapter missing --dry-run. Added scripts/robustness_tests.py (wired into CPU regression, all PASS). Framing downgraded: (a) task near-deterministic from trace booleans (3-line rule = 9/9 private eval); (b) strongest baseline plateau_then_w TIES selector on regret (0 vs 0, 6/9 W) on held-out eval -> selector edge is nominal/outcome-neutral lever-accuracy only; (c) gpt-oss/LoRA result NOT RUN (GPU-blocked) -> do not quote. Report: documentation/adversarial_review.md.

kernel_task_2026_06_06.what: kernel_task/ — SIA H-vs-W lever on the paper's GPU-kernel task (TriMul / Triangle Multiplicative Update). Agent writes a kernel; weak verifier (fixed input) passes a fast-but-WRONG kernel; W-only entrenches it; H (strengthen verifier to random inputs) + retrain repairs. Label-free selector picks H_THEN_W from the implausible-speedup shortcut signature.
kernel_task_2026_06_06.status: BUILT + CPU-verified end-to-end (stub agent). GPU-ready: swap stub->gpt-oss endpoint + Triton seed + CUDA timing. Only model calls + triton need hardware.
kernel_task_2026_06_06.cpu_result: W_only final correct=False (fast-but-wrong, ~80x bogus speedup); H_only exposes not repairs; H_THEN_W + selector correct=True. test_cpu_stub.py ALL PASS.
kernel_task_2026_06_06.files: kernel_task/{reference,runner(subprocess-isolated),harness(weak/strong verifier),agent(stub+endpoint),lever_loop(selector),run,check_env}.py, seeds/{naive_loops,torch_bmm,memorize_cheat,triton_trimul}.py, prompts/, tests/test_cpu_stub.py; scripts/run_kernel_comparison.sh; triton added to requirements-gpu.txt.
kernel_task_2026_06_06.gpu_run: python kernel_task/check_env.py && bash scripts/run_kernel_comparison.sh --endpoint  (needs H100/H200+triton + GPT_OSS_BASE_URL/MODEL/KEY)
kernel_task_2026_06_06.honest_scope: matches the paper TASK (TriMul Triton), NOT the 14x RESULT. Report measured speedups. CPU stub agent is deterministic (proves harness/loop); whether gpt-oss actually cheats under the weak verifier needs the endpoint. triton_trimul seed is correct-but-unoptimized.

headroom_gate_2026_06_06.summary: Cheap CPU pre-flight before the GPU (Phase 4) rung: trace_difficulty_probe.py measures whether a tiny threshold model already solves the task on held-out (=no headroom for a 120B).
headroom_gate_2026_06_06.original_task_verdict: NO HEADROOM — tiny 2-feature rule = 1.00 held-out even with giveaway booleans stripped (modes threshold-separable in raw measurement space). Do NOT spend H200 on it.

hard_task.builder: experiments/hard_task.py (continuous severity + compound faults + seed noise; ground truth from real reruns, no transition table)
hard_task.verdict: INFORMATIVE — headroom band ~[0.47,0.81]; tiny rule + original hand rule cannot cross it; compound faults break single-axis thresholds. Phase 4 base-vs-LoRA here would be a real attribution result.

headroom_gate_2026_06_06.gate_tool: python experiments/trace_difficulty_probe.py --cache <cache> --eval-seeds <n>  -> prints NO HEADROOM / INFORMATIVE
headroom_gate_2026_06_06.turnkey_gpu_cmd: CACHE=gpt_oss/data/out/hard_cache.jsonl bash scripts/run_gpu_comparison.sh --model $GPT_OSS_MODEL --base-url $GPT_OSS_BASE_URL  (--cache plumbed through ALL builders/rollout/eval/compare; step 3b = headroom gate; verified end-to-end on CPU dry-run)
headroom_gate_2026_06_06.report_metric: LEVER ACCURACY (not regret): on hard, plateau_then_w games regret by peeking at measured reward (~0.016). Trace-only learnable signal = accuracy; floor 0.47, ceiling 0.81. Old oracle_sandwich rule = 0.29 on hard (INVALID baseline here) -> build a new rule or report base-vs-LoRA accuracy.
headroom_gate_2026_06_06.learner_ceiling: experiments/learner_ceiling_probe.py: tiny trained MLP on hard trace = 0.58 eval (floor 0.46, ceiling 0.81) -> a small model can't close the gap, so hard task is a LEGITIMATE learning problem (easy task MLP = 1.00).
headroom_gate_2026_06_06.prompt_leak_FIX: CRITICAL pre-GPU: default selector prompt (lever_selector_system.md) SPELLS OUT the decision rule (principles 1-4) -> base gpt-oss-120b would follow the recipe and near-solve zero-shot -> base-vs-LoRA = guaranteed NULL. Added rule-HIDDEN variant lever_selector_system_blind.md (env LEVER_PROMPT=blind). Run the GPU experiment with LEVER_PROMPT=blind so 'learned attribution' is real, not instruction-following.
headroom_gate_2026_06_06.recommended_gpu_protocol: 1) LEVER_PROMPT=blind, CACHE=hard. 2) base rollout -> base accuracy (vs MLP 0.58, ceiling 0.81). 3) if base >= ~0.78 STOP (base already attributes zero-shot; report that). 4) else LoRA SFT(+DPO) -> eval_adapter on ACCURACY. 5) result = LoRA vs base vs tiny-MLP(0.58) vs ceiling(0.81), rule-hidden prompt.
headroom_gate_2026_06_06.fit_assessment: 120B is OVERKILL on capacity (task = 4-number text classification; justified only by paper-alignment). Defensible as a learning testbed ONLY with rule-hidden prompt + hard task; otherwise it measures instruction-following. Cheapest honest result may be base-zero-shot (no LoRA).
headroom_gate_2026_06_06.next_if_gpu: 1) base rollout on hard -> measure base gpt-oss-120b lever accuracy (is it already near 0.81?). 2) only if base << ceiling, train LoRA (SFT then DPO). 3) eval_adapter base-vs-LoRA on ACCURACY. 4) report movement within [0.47,0.81], never as 'perfect attribution'.

adversarial_audit_2026_06_06.verdict: CPU core sound + reproduced; framing corrected; gpt-oss/LoRA lane unvalidated (GPU-blocked).
framing_downgrades: task near-deterministic from trace (3-line rule 9/9 private eval)
plateau_then_w ties selector on eval regret (0 vs 0); edge is nominal lever-accuracy only
gpt-oss+LoRA NOT RUN; remove 'LoRA improves over base' as a result
